traps.description: `setQuery` snapshots `this.page` into the debounced callback via a local `capturedPage` const, then reads that snapshot when the timer fires. If `setPage` is invoked inside the debounce window the page change is silently lost — the timer issues a fetch for the page the user has already left. The callback should read `this.page` (and `this.query`) freshly when it fires, not at scheduling time.

`_runFetch` writes `users` and `total` from whichever fetch resolves last, with no notion of which call is current. A slow fetch for an older query (or older page) that resolves after a faster newer fetch will clobber the fresh results. Fix needs a request generation counter, an AbortController, or some other mechanism that drops stale responses on commit.

`getState().totalPages = Math.floor(total / pageSize)` hides the last partial page — e.g. 11 users at pageSize 5 reports 2 pages instead of 3, so page 3 is unreachable from the UI. Should be `Math.ceil(total / pageSize)` (with a sensible fallback when `total === 0`).
